objective: Define the minimum private worker API needed to move libTV, ffmpeg, long-running video generation, and result import away from the local desktop path before public real-generation launch.

secretRules: Do not expose LIBTV_WORKER_TOKEN to browser, PWA, or Capacitor WebView code.
Do not return local filesystem paths, database paths, provider keys, or storage credentials to public clients.
Only the API service may translate worker resultRefs into user-visible downloads or previews.

errorCodes.meaning: The task payload is missing required product assets, prompt package data, or generation options.
The worker cannot read one or more API-provided asset references.
libTV or the configured provider rejected the task for compliance reasons.
The downstream AI/video provider failed after the worker accepted the task.
The worker, libTV runtime, ffmpeg, storage, or database layer failed unexpectedly.
The task exceeded the configured wait or generation time limit.
The API requested cancellation before the task completed.

idempotency.rule: POST /tasks must return the existing workerTaskId when the same taskId is submitted again by the API.

storageContract.inputRefs: API-owned references under RUN_STORAGE_DIR or signed internal references readable by the worker.
storageContract.resultRefs: Opaque references returned to the API service only. Public clients never receive worker-local paths.
storageContract.retention: Follow the production retention policy recorded in owner inputs before public launch.

statusCodes.409: task cannot be accepted because the existing task has conflicting payload
task cannot be cancelled in current state

apiIntegrationRules: The Node API keeps the user-facing task ID and maps it to workerTaskId server-side.
Mobile notifications and task cards must show normalized statuses only: queued, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled.
Worker errorCode values must be mapped to user-readable Chinese messages before reaching the mobile UI.
The API should poll worker status for pilot release; callbacks can be added later after network routing is stable.
Real generation remains gated by LIBTV_DEFAULT_DRY_RUN=false only after worker health, storage restart, and rollback checks pass.

acceptanceChecks.evidence: Contract schema, endpoints, auth, status enum, and failure mappings are valid.
Worker/storage migration plan references this contract.
Production environment no longer points to localhost or Windows worker paths.
Public HTTPS deployment and authenticated API smoke pass.
